items.headline: RBI Injects ₹1.41 Lakh Crore to Ease Banking Liquidity
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) injected ₹1.41 lakh crore (approximately $17 billion) into the banking system through a seven-day variable rate repo (VRR) auction to alleviate a liquidity deficit, the first recorded since March 2026, primarily due to advance tax payments.

items.headline: India's May Inflation Rises to 3.9%, Rainfall Deficit Raises Concerns
items.summary: India's inflation rose to 3.9% in May, the highest in 16 months, although still below the RBI's 4% target. However, persistent food inflation, elevated fuel costs, and a significant rainfall deficit (40%) could keep consumer confidence subdued, with June inflation potentially reaching 4.2% and 5.8-6.0% in H2 FY27.

items.headline: Government Initiates OFS for IRFC, Revises FEMA Rules for G-Sec Market
items.summary: The Indian government has initiated an Offer for Sale (OFS) to divest up to a 2% stake in Indian Railway Finance Corporation (IRFC) at a floor price of ₹91 per share. Additionally, the Ministry of Finance and RBI revised FEMA rules for non-debt instruments to deepen the Government Securities market and boost FPI participation.

items.headline: US Tech Sell-off Drags Global Markets, Crude Oil Prices Decline
items.summary: US equities declined (S&P 500 -1.4%, Nasdaq -2.2%) due to a broad tech sell-off and hawkish Fed expectations. Asian markets were mixed, but a positive development for India is the continued decline in crude oil prices (Brent around $76-$77/barrel) due to progress in US-Iran peace talks, offering some support.

items.headline: Chambal Breweries, Vikas Lifecare to Announce Results; TCS Q1 on July 9
items.summary: Chambal Breweries & Distilleries Ltd, Vikas Lifecare, and Ashwini Container Movers are scheduled to announce their audited financial results today, June 24, 2026. Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) is slated to declare its Q1 FY27 results and an interim dividend decision on July 9, 2026.

items.headline: Indian Market Sees Broad Sell-off; Pharma and Healthcare Outperform
items.summary: The Indian equity market experienced a broad-based sell-off on June 23, with Sensex and Nifty 50 declining over 1%. Metals, IT, Financial Services, PSU Banks, Realty, and Consumer Durables sectors witnessed significant weakness. In contrast, the Pharmaceutical and Healthcare sectors showed relative resilience and outperformed, suggesting defensive buying.

items.headline: Government to Divest 2% Stake in IRFC via OFS
items.summary: The government is selling up to a 2% stake (26.1 crore shares) in Indian Railway Finance Corporation (IRFC) through an Offer for Sale (OFS) at a floor price of ₹91 per share, with non-retail bidding commencing on June 24.

items.headline: Honasa Consumer Acquires Majority Stake in Fluence Pharma for ₹135 Crore
items.summary: Honasa Consumer is set to acquire a 58% majority stake in nutraceuticals firm Fluence Pharma for an enterprise value of ₹135 crore, marking its entry into the nutraceuticals segment.

items.headline: Rashi Peripherals Acquires 67% Stake in VDA Infosolutions
items.summary: Rashi Peripherals has signed definitive agreements to acquire a 67% stake in VDA Infosolutions for ₹368.50 crore, with plans to acquire the remaining 33% by August 2029.

items.headline: HPCL Announces Commercial Operation of Rajasthan Refinery
items.summary: Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) announced the Commercial Operation Date (COD) of its mega Rajasthan Refinery on June 22.

items.headline: Infosys Expands Collaboration with GlobalFoundries for AI-led Managed Services
items.summary: Infosys entered an expanded multi-year collaboration with GlobalFoundries for AI-led managed services.

items.headline: PI Industries Strengthens Pharma Intermediate Segment Equity
items.summary: PI Industries strengthened the equity structure of its pharma intermediate segment by converting ₹1,000 crore worth of Optionally Fully Convertible Debentures (OFCDs) in its subsidiary, PI Health Sciences, into 72.46 crore fresh equity shares.

items.headline: Vedanta Promoter Twin Star Holdings Sells 1.66% Stake for ₹1,895 Crore
items.summary: Vedanta promoter entity Twin Star Holdings sold 6.5 crore shares, representing a 1.66% stake, for ₹1,895.96 crore through a bulk deal.

items.headline: Nexus Venture Partners Sells 0.57% Stake in Delhivery
items.summary: Private equity firm Nexus Venture Partners sold over 43 lakh shares (0.57% stake) in Delhivery for ₹208 crore through a bulk deal.

items.headline: Craftsman Automation Promoter Sells 2% Stake for ₹485 Crore
items.summary: Promoter Srinivasan Ravi sold 5.25 lakh shares (2% stake) in Craftsman Automation for ₹485.62 crore via a bulk deal.

items.headline: YES Bank Board to Consider Fundraising Plans on June 29
items.summary: YES Bank's board is scheduled to meet on June 29 to discuss potential fundraising plans.

items.headline: Wipro Acquisition of Alpha Net Consulting Customer Contracts Revised to September
items.summary: The acquisition of select Alpha Net Consulting LLC customer contracts by Wipro is now expected by September 30, 2026, revising the earlier June 30, 2026, estimate.

items.headline: GTPL Hathway to Acquire ACT Group's Cable Television Business
items.summary: GTPL Hathway will acquire the cable television business of ACT Group for ₹36.23 crore.

items.headline: Tata Power's Mundra Plant Mandated Full Capacity; Signs MoU for RE Projects
items.summary: The Power Ministry has mandated Tata Power's 4,150 MW Mundra plant to operate at full capacity for an additional three months until September. The company also signed an MoU for large-scale Renewable Energy (RE) projects in Tamil Nadu.

items.headline: CSM Technologies IPO Opens Today, Aims to Raise ₹145.78 Crore
items.summary: The Initial Public Offering (IPO) for CSM Technologies opened today, June 24, 2026. It is a book-build issue of ₹145.78 crore, entirely comprising fresh equity.

items.headline: IRFC, YES Bank, Infosys, Tata Power, JSW Steel, Tata Steel in Focus
items.summary: Stocks including IRFC, YES Bank, Infosys, Honasa Consumer, Tata Power, JSW Steel, Tata Steel, Delhivery, and Wipro are expected to be closely watched today due to various corporate actions and sectoral trends. JSW Steel and Tata Steel are also in focus as India initiated an anti-dumping probe against electrical steel imports.
